summary: A Python programming app built with React + Motoko. Since the platform doesn't run Python natively, the app will focus on Python programming concepts, code examples, and learning/practice features.
architectural_notes: Platform is React + Motoko; Python cannot be executed natively — app should focus on Python learning/display content
imageRequirements:
## Required Images
- Stylized Python snake coiled around angle brackets (< >) with a glowing green tint on a dark charcoal background (pylearn-logo.dim_128x128.png)

existing_features.title: Build a lesson browser page showing all available Python lessons as cards. Each card displays the lesson title, topic tag, difficulty badge, and a completion checkmark if the user has completed it. Include a filter bar to filter lessons by difficulty level (All, Beginner, Intermediate, Advanced).

Build a lesson detail view that opens when a user clicks a lesson card. The detail view displays the full lesson description and a syntax-highlighted Python code snippet using a monospace code block with line numbers. Include a 'Mark as Complete' button that updates the user's progress in the backend.

Apply a cohesive dark-themed visual design inspired by code editors: deep charcoal/dark slate backgrounds, bright green or amber accent colors for highlights and CTAs, monospace fonts for code blocks, and a clean sans-serif font for UI text. Use consistent card layouts, subtle borders, and glowing accent effects on interactive elements.
Generate and display a logo image for the app header: a stylized Python snake icon intertwined with code brackets on a dark background, used as the app's branding mark in the top navigation bar.

new_feature_requests.summary: Python programming learning app with code examples, exercises, and interactive Python content

Create a backend actor that stores Python programming lessons, each with a title, description, code snippet (string), difficulty level (Beginner/Intermediate/Advanced), and a unique ID. Provide functions to: list all lessons, get a lesson by ID, and add new lessons. Pre-populate with at least 8 diverse Python lessons covering topics such as variables, loops, functions, list comprehensions, dictionaries, classes, error handling, and file I/O.
Create a backend actor function to store and retrieve user progress: which lesson IDs the user has marked as completed. Provide markComplete(lessonId) and getProgress() functions.
